title: Peter Gibbons' Mundane Life
description: Peter's routine office life is boring, monotonous and soul-crushing. He has very little autonomy or motivation and is stuck in a dead-end job at Initech.

title: Initech hires the consultants
description: Initech announces that it has brought in outside consultants to evaluate the company's operations, with the clear implication that layoffs are coming. For Peter, this disrupts even the grim comfort of his routine — the system he has been sleepwalking through may no longer have a place for him at all.

title: Peter is caught by Lumbergh
description: Peter tries to sneak out of the office on Friday afternoon, but his computer takes too long to shut down and Lumbergh catches him before he can escape. Despite his growing awareness that a different life is possible, Peter is still far from being able to act on it, and quietly accepts yet another weekend of work.

title: Peter gets hypnotized
description: Peter undergoes hypnotherapy at the suggestion of his partner Anne. Dr. Swanson (the therapist) places him into a deep state of relaxation, providing him with a form of aid that strips away the anxiety and obedience that have defined his entire adult life.

title: Dr. Swanson's heart attack
description: Dr. Swanson dies of a heart attack before he can bring Peter out of his trance. What was meant to be a temporary intervention becomes a permanent transformation, and Peter crosses fully into a new state of being with no way back.

title: Peter sleeps through the morning
description: Peter sleeps peacefully as calls from Initech pile up on his answering machine. He is now completely detached from the world that once controlled every aspect of his life, fully swallowed by whatever the hypnotherapy has made him into.

title: Peter talks to Anne
description: When Anne calls to confront Peter about his behavior, he responds with flat indifference, leading her to break up with him over voicemail. While this moment appears liberating, Peter's reasoning is entirely surface-level — he simply doesn't care, rather than having any deeper understanding of what he actually wants or who he is becoming.

title: Peter's Date with Joanna
description: Peter takes Joanna to a restaurant, where the two bond over a shared hatred of their jobs and the petty indignities of corporate life. In Joanna, Peter experiences a form of unconditional acceptance that inspires him to continue being the honest, unburdened person he has started to become.

title: Peter's meeting with the Bobs
description: During his meeting with the corporate consultants both named Bob, Peter offers a series of honest, unfiltered responses that reveal his true feelings about his job, his boss, and his life. In response, the Bobs indirectly offer a promotion and enhanced compensation. Although Peter doesn't explicitly reject the idea, his muted response shows that he effectively rejects the temptation to fall back into a frame of mind based entirely in corporate success and material gain.

title: Peter learns about Joanna and Lumbergh
description: Peter discovers that Joanna may have slept with Lumbergh, and is devastated. By this point, Peter has openly defied Lumbergh in nearly every way — ignoring him in the hallway, gutting a fish at his desk, tearing down his own cubicle wall. Yet this single revelation undoes all of that defiance, proving that Lumbergh still holds a deep, almost primal power over Peter's sense of self.

title: Peter admits the truth to Joanna
description: Peter meets with Joanna and confesses that he was wrong to judge her, acknowledging the hypocrisy she had called out in him. In this moment, the apathetic, disengaged person Peter was at the start of the story is fully gone.

title: Peter's confession
description: Peter writes a confession letter and slides it under Lumbergh's door along with the stolen money. In doing so, he demonstrates a degree of honesty and personal accountability that he was completely incapable of at the start of the story.

title: Peter tries to take back the confession
description: Immediately after sliding the confession under Lumbergh's door, Peter drops to the floor and desperately tries to pull it back. Having just demonstrated complete moral honesty, he immediately recoils from the consequences of his own growth.

title: Milton burns down Initech
description: Milton sets fire to the Initech building, destroying the confession letter and all evidence of the theft. The very system that once controlled Peter's life is consumed entirely, clearing his path to a new one.

title: Lawrence gets Peter into Construction
description: Lawrence brings Peter into his construction crew, providing him with a path forward after the destruction of Initech. Through this simple act, Lawrence rescues Peter from the uncertainty of his situation and delivers him into the kind of honest, physical work that suits his transformed self.

title: Peter meets with Samir and Michael
description: Michael and Samir visit Peter at the construction site to offer him a job at their new software company. Peter politely declines, choosing to remain in the fulfilling, physical work that reflects the person he has become.

title: Peter keeps Milton's stapler
description: While clearing the rubble of the Initech building, Peter finds Milton's red Swingline stapler and sets it aside. In keeping it, Peter shows that he still understands what matters to the people of his former world, even as he has moved well beyond it.

title: Peter lives freely
description: Peter works alongside Lawrence on the construction site, clearing the rubble of the Initech building. He is content, present, and free to live on his own terms.
